channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Government outlines key reforms to strengthen Tanzania’s cooperative sector
item.description: The Government of Tanzania has identified five major challenges that continue to slow the growth and effectiveness of the country’s cooperative sector. Speaking during the Cooperative Day celebrations...

item.title: Russian missile and drone strikes on Kyiv kill at least five as Ukraine renews calls for stronger air defences
item.description: Russia launched a large-scale overnight attack on Kyiv, firing waves of ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, and drones at the Ukrainian capital. According to local authorities, at least five people w...

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo's National Anthem Stance Explained
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo, the Portuguese football legend, has long been observed standing sideways during the national anthem before international matches. This unique posture, which he has maintained throug...

item.title: Senator Rodante Marcoleta Faces Arrest Over Alleged P75 Million Campaign Donation
item.description: The Sandiganbayan's 3rd Division issued an arrest warrant and hold-departure order against Senator Rodante Marcoleta and three co-accused in a plunder case involving allegations of P75 million in camp...

item.title: Southern California Heat Exacerbates Poor Air Quality Amid Fireworks and Wildfire Smoke
item.description: The intense heatwave gripping Southern California is worsening an already dire air quality crisis, according to experts. A high-pressure system causing record temperatures is also suppressing the disp...

item.title: UK, Japan, and Italy collaborate on sixth-gen fighter jet development
item.description: The UK, Japan, and Italy have signed a $6.14 billion contract with the joint venture Edgewing to advance the design of a sixth-generation fighter jet as part of the Global Combat Air Programme (GCAP)....

item.title: Engineer Faces Legal Battle Over Destroying Surveillance Cameras
item.description: Jeffrey Sovern, an engineer, faces criminal charges for allegedly destroying Flock surveillance cameras, sparking public debate over privacy rights and corporate overreach. The incident has garnered w...

item.title: Pitso Mosimane Supports Relebohile Mofokeng's Move to Belgian Club
item.description: Relebohile Mofokeng, the 21-year-old South African footballer, has completed a transfer to Belgian side Royale Union Saint-Gilloise, marking a significant step in his European career. Pitso Mosimane, ...

item.title: Gilgit-Baltistan CM-elect to be sworn in amid cabinet planning and re-polling orders
item.description: Advocate Amjad Hussain, the chief minister-elect of Gilgit-Baltistan, will take the oath of office on Monday after his swearing-in ceremony was postponed from July 1. The delay occurred because PPP ch...

item.title: Manipur Updates Electoral Roll with 19.34 Lakh Voters
item.description: The Chief Electoral Officer of Manipur, Arun Kumar Sinha, announced that the state has 19.34 lakh voters in the draft electoral roll following the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. ...

item.title: Banks and E-Wallets Lower Digital Transfer Fees in Response to BSP Guidelines
item.description: The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) has mandated financial institutions to reduce digital transfer fees, prompting several banks and e-wallets to adjust their rates. Circular No. 1238 requires insti...

item.title: Planned Parenthood Regains Medicaid Funding After Defunding Law Expires, But Challenges Remain
item.description: The provision in the One Big Beautiful Bill Act that defunded Planned Parenthood and other reproductive health clinics expired, restoring Medicaid funding access. This ended a one-year defunding perio...

item.title: South Africa declines Nigeria's request for compensation over properties abandoned during xenophobic unrest
item.description: The South African government has rejected Nigeria's request for compensation on behalf of Nigerians who left businesses, homes and other properties behind while fleeing renewed xenophobic tensions in ...

item.title: Cape Verde's remarkable 2026 World Cup debut and the future of its diaspora-powered team
item.description: Cape Verde, a small West African island nation with a population of just over half a million, made an impressive debut at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Ranked 67th in the world, the Blue Sharks advanced fr...

item.title: Mariners extend Seattle's scoreless streak with 4-0 win over Blue Jays
item.description: The Seattle Mariners secured a 4-0 victory over the Toronto Blue Jays, extending their scoreless streak to 24 innings. Emerson Hancock (6-4) pitched seven shutout innings, allowing just two hits and t...

item.title: Thunderstorms threaten kick-off as England prepare to face Mexico in World Cup last-16 tie
item.description: Severe thunderstorms in Mexico City dominated the build-up to England's World Cup last-16 match against Mexico at the Azteca Stadium, raising concerns that the scheduled kick-off could be delayed. Loc...

item.title: Big Bear bald eagle parents returned to their nest after Fourth of July fireworks, with eaglets remaining safe
item.description: Jackie and Shadow, the well-known bald eagle pair that nest near Big Bear Lake in California, temporarily left their nesting area during the local Fourth of July fireworks display but returned the fol...

item.title: Sindh Minister Addresses HIV Outbreak at Valika Hospital
item.description: Sindh Labour Minister Saeed Ghani confirmed that at least 78 children were infected with HIV/AIDS at Kulsoom Bai Valika Hospital, a facility under the Sindh Employees’ Social Security Institution (SES...

item.title: Naomi Osaka defeats Aryna Sabalenka in Wimbledon quarterfinals
item.description: Naomi Osaka delivered a major upset at Wimbledon by defeating top-seeded Aryna Sabalenka in the quarterfinals, sending the Belarusian world number one packing. The match, which took place on July 6, 2...

item.title: Swae Lee Performs at 2026 Hollywoodbets Durban July
item.description: Grammy-nominated American artist Swae Lee captivated thousands at the 2026 Hollywoodbets Durban July, delivering a high-energy performance that highlighted his status as a global hitmaker. Returning t...
